preview_text:
The model rates Argentina as the overwhelming favorite with a 76% chance of victory, leaving Austria just 4% to win. That asymmetry reflects a substantial quality gap in the forecast, though the underdog retains a non-trivial probability of an upset.

The win-draw-loss spread shows Argentina heavily favored but with meaningful draw probability at 20%. A stalemate is plausible enough to influence match strategy, yet the model's central case strongly leans toward an Argentine win rather than a competitive equilibrium. The gap between the favorite and underdog is wide rather than closely matched.

The model and market diverge meaningfully. Sportsbooks offer Argentina only 60%, roughly 16 percentage points lower than the model, while assigning Austria 16%—four times the model's estimate. Polymarket pricing falls between these two but sits closer to the books. The dashboard does not claim to know which source is right; the gap suggests material disagreement on Argentina's true advantage.
